verdict_reason: BG 2.9 is pure narration with no doctrinally contested terms — Sanjaya reports Arjuna's 'na yotsya iti' and his subsequent silence. The engineering layer treats the verse as scaffolding without claiming doctrine. There is no tradition-specific reading to attribute and none is attributed; there are no load-bearing technical terms (yajña, dharma, ātman, etc.) to drop. The cross-traditional consensus is that this verse 'has no doctrinal content of its own' (per the source pack's traditional_meaning_consensus), and the engineering layer mirrors this consensus precisely. PASS.
